title: Collecting Duckweed Samples For Microbial Community Profiling
author: Kenneth Acosta
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.98zh9x6

steps.title: Get 150 mL 0
steps.instruction: Get 150 mL 0.2 micron filter unit. Lay sterilized miracloth on top of filter unit.

steps.title: Collect 150-300 mL of water sample...
steps.instruction: Collect 150-300 mL of water sample and pour through sterilized Miracloth into filter unit.

steps.title: Filter water sample through 0
steps.instruction: Filter water sample through 0.2 micron filter unit by turning vaccum on.

steps.title: Cut 0
steps.instruction: Cut 0.2 micron filter from unit using sterile scalpel.

steps.title: Flash freeze and store at -80C until processing
steps.instruction: Flash freeze and store at -80C until processing.

steps.title: Collect 1-3 grams of fresh weight Duckweed from environment sample
steps.instruction: Collect 1-3 grams of fresh weight Duckweed from environment sample. (See “Separating Duckweed From Other Solids” to obtain pure Duckweed tissue from environment.)

steps.title: Rinse tissue 3-5x’s with 30-40 mL rinse solution
steps.instruction: Rinse tissue 3-5x’s with 30-40 mL rinse solution. Filter rinse solution through 0.2 micron filter each time.

steps.title: Filter a total of 100-150 mL...
steps.instruction: Filter a total of 100-150 mL of rinse solution through 0.2 micron filter.

steps.title: Cut filter using sterile scalpel, transfer...
steps.instruction: Cut filter using sterile scalpel, transfer to 5 mL centrifuge tube, flash freeze, and store for 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing.

steps.title: Wash rinsed Duckweed tissue 2x’s with sterile water
steps.instruction: Wash rinsed Duckweed tissue 2x’s with sterile water.

steps.title: Transfer tissue to 5 mL centrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Transfer tissue to 5 mL centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Flash freeze and store for 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing
steps.instruction: Flash freeze and store for 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing.
